title: West Nile Virus found in mosquito test sample in Jamaica Plain
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: West Nile Virus detected in a Jamaica Plain mosquito sample, the first positive this summer in Boston, BPHC reports.
Residents advised to mitigate mosquito bite risk through repellents, draining water, and wearing protective clothing outdoors.
BPHC and Suffolk County Mosquito Control Project to monitor and collect mosquito samples weekly in Boston.

title: Boston man accused of making homemade explosives, threats on MBTA Commuter Rail
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Shane Cautillo, 29, charged for making bomb threats and carrying homemade explosives on MBTA Commuter Rail, officials report.
Cautillo allegedly possessed a disconnected lithium battery, an electric scooter with fireworks, and an electric towel warmer with suspicious contents.
Cautillo held on $10,000 bail, prohibited from using MBTA transit; scheduled for a pretrial hearing on July 28.

title: Lawsuit: ICE detained East Boston father despite legal status
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Jose Pineda, a 62-year-old Salvadoran immigrant with legal U.S. status, sues ICE for unlawful detention and racial profiling.
Pineda was detained in May 2025 for two days in ICE's Burlington facility under allegedly harsh conditions, causing severe distress.
The lawsuit seeks damages under the Federal Tort Claims Act, alleging false arrest, imprisonment, and assault by ICE agents.

title: Patriots 2026 position preview: Running backs edition
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Rhamondre Stevenson and TreVeyon Henderson lead the Patriots' running back group, which ranked sixth in NFL rushing yards per game.
Henderson set a franchise rookie record with 5.1 yards per carry, but struggled in the postseason averaging 2.5 yards per carry.
Depth concerns exist with the departure of Antonio Gibson and little new offseason additions beyond draft pick Jam Miller.

title: Boston cyclist, a city employee, hit and killed by vehicle on Tremont Street
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A cyclist was fatally struck on Tremont Street in Mission Hill, Boston, on Thursday morning.
The victim, Louisa Gag, 36, was a Boston city employee; she died at the scene.
The incident closed Tremont Street from Brigham Circle to Roxbury Crossing until noon.

title: Massachusetts Senate passes bill targeting addictive social media features for kids
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: The Massachusetts Senate passed a bill targeting addictive social media features designed to engage minors compulsively.
The bill requires social media platforms to disable autoplay, infinite scroll, and other features by default for minors.
The legislation mandates annual reports from social media companies detailing how they engage and target minors.

title: Police seize dog who severely injured smaller pup in Chelsea attack caught on video
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Chelsea police have seized a larger dog involved in a violent attack on a smaller dog Monday night on Chestnut Street.
The injured dog, a service animal named Snoopy Snoop Dog, requires leg amputation following the attack.
Investigations are underway to assess legal violations, and a dangerous dog hearing is being considered.

title: Mac Jones opens up about his tumultuous time with the Patriots
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Mac Jones described his time with the Patriots as tumultuous, citing clashes with Bill Belichick and coaching changes.
Jones highlighted confusion during practices as offensive responsibilities were split between Matt Patricia and Joe Judge.
Jones was traded to Jacksonville for a sixth-round pick, feeling it was time to start anew after struggles in New England.

title: 5 takeaways as France defeat Morocco in the last ‘Boston Stadium’ World Cup game, advance to semifinals
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: France defeated Morocco 2-0 in the World Cup quarterfinal at Gillette Stadium, advancing to the semifinals.
Goals by Kylian Mbappe and Ousmane Dembélé secured the victory for France against a resilient Moroccan team.
The game marks the end of World Cup matches at 'Boston Stadium', with France progressing to face Spain or Belgium.

title: ‘He hit me’: Pregame France vs. Morocco press conference briefly halted after journalist altercation
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Journalists clashed during a France vs. Morocco pregame press conference at Boston Stadium, causing a temporary halt.
The dispute involved accusations of physical contact between journalists, though the exact details remain unclear.
Moroccan player Brahim Díaz joked about forgetting his question after the altercation ended.
